evidence_quote: Lord Walder had ordered the slaughter of the Starks at Roslin's wedding; watches the slaughter 'greedily' from his throne; mocks Robb: 'Seems we killed some of your men, Your Grace.'
rationale: Walder Frey ordered the slaughter and watched it from his throne but did not personally execute any victims.

evidence_quote: Tywin Lannister countenanced the massacre (additional context); 'Jaime Lannister sends his regards' invoked at the killing blow, implying Lannister involvement.
rationale: Tywin Lannister countenanced the Red Wedding but there is no direct quote of him issuing the order, only strong inference of his blessing.

evidence_quote: 'Lame Lothar who had plotted it out with Roose Bolton'; Merrett names him as one of the actual plotters: 'his father, Ryman, Bolton, Lothar, Bastard Walder.'
rationale: Lame Lothar co-plotted the massacre with Roose Bolton but did not personally execute any victims.

evidence_quote: 'A man in dark armor and a pale pink cloak spotted with blood steps up and says Jaime Lannister sends his regards. He thrusts his longsword through Robb's heart and twists.'
rationale: Roose Bolton personally drove his longsword through Robb Stark's heart, physically executing the killing blow, and so qualifies as agent rather than commander.

evidence_quote: 'Ser Ryman kills Dacey Mormont — He buries his axe head in her stomach.'
rationale: Ser Ryman Frey physically buried his axe in Dacey Mormont's stomach, directly executing her death.

evidence_quote: 'Lucas Blackwood is cut down — Killed by Ser Hosteen Frey.'
rationale: Ser Hosteen Frey personally cut down Lucas Blackwood during the massacre.

evidence_quote: Raymund Frey cut Catelyn's throat (additional context); 'Someone grabs her scalp and cuts her throat.'
rationale: Raymund Frey personally cut Catelyn Stark's throat, directly executing her death.

evidence_quote: 'Black Walder | Hamstrings | One of the Vances | During the massacre.'
rationale: Black Walder physically hamstrung one of the Vances during the massacre, making him a direct executor.

evidence_quote: 'Musicians in the gallery have crossbows instead of instruments'; 'Crossbows kill more northmen — Donnel Locke, Owen Norrey, and half a dozen others fall.'
rationale: Frey crossbowmen hidden as musicians in the gallery opened fire, killing Robb and numerous northmen, constituting the primary mass-execution force.

evidence_quote: Bolton men-at-arms killed numerous northmen in the camps (additional context); Bolton soldiers identified by 'pale pink cloak with red teardrops' and 'flayed man badge.'
rationale: Bolton men-at-arms participated in the slaughter of Robb's forces during the massacre.

evidence_quote: 'A crossbow bolt sprouts from his side beneath the shoulder. A second pierces his leg.' Then: 'He thrusts his longsword through Robb's heart and twists.'
rationale: Robb Stark was the primary target of the Red Wedding, shot with crossbow bolts and then slain by Roose Bolton's sword through his heart.

evidence_quote: 'Catelyn is killed — Someone grabs her scalp and cuts her throat.'; 'something hits her in the small of the back; she falls to the floor.'
rationale: Catelyn Stark was shot, then had her throat cut by Raymund Frey, dying as a victim of the massacre.

evidence_quote: 'Ser Ryman kills Dacey Mormont — He buries his axe head in her stomach.'
rationale: Dacey Mormont was killed by Ser Ryman Frey's axe during the massacre while serving as one of Robb's guards.

evidence_quote: The Red Wedding took place during Edmure Tully's wedding feast at the Twins (additional context); Arya and the Hound 'continue toward the Twins at evenfall.'
rationale: The Red Wedding took place during Edmure Tully's wedding feast held at the Twins, the Frey seat.

evidence_quote: Lady Olenna fussed with Sansa's hair net (tucking, straightening); one black amethyst went missing with only a dark smudge in the socket — additional context confirms she physically introduced the poison into the wine.
rationale: Lady Olenna physically extracted the poison stone from Sansa's hair net and introduced the Strangler into Joffrey's wine during the feast.

evidence_quote: "All he did he did at my behest"; "Littlefinger orchestrated the plot, using Dontos Hollard as a catspaw."
rationale: Littlefinger orchestrated the entire assassination plot — procuring the Strangler, concealing it in Sansa's hair net, and recruiting Dontos as his catspaw — without personally introducing the poison.

evidence_quote: "Dontos was always his catspaw, recruited after Sansa saved Dontos's life at Joffrey's tourney"; he guided Sansa through the escape route and called her 'sweet Jonquil.'
rationale: Dontos physically delivered the murder weapon (the hair net containing the Strangler) to Sansa and guided her to the feast, acting as Littlefinger's knowing catspaw even if unaware of the hair net's exact contents.

evidence_quote: "Pulling the silver hair net free from her auburn hair, she finds one black amethyst missing, with only a dark smudge in the socket. She is seized by terror, suspecting the stone was used in Joffrey's death."
rationale: Sansa unwittingly carried the poison-laced hair net to the wedding feast, providing the physical vehicle for the Strangler's delivery; she had no knowledge of its lethal purpose.

evidence_quote: "Joffrey dies — His eyes meet Tyrion's. He lifts a hand toward Tyrion — reaching or pointing. A fearful thin sound stops. Tommen screams and cries."
rationale: Joffrey was poisoned with the Strangler at his own wedding feast and died in public view.

evidence_quote: Feast events described within the Red Keep; Sansa escapes via boat from the keep's walls after the poisoning.
rationale: The wedding feast — where Joffrey was poisoned — took place in the throne room of the Red Keep, after the ceremony at the Great Sept.

evidence_quote: Catelyn swears 'Stannis killed Renly through sorcery'; the shadow with immaterial blade cuts through Renly's gorget — the sorcery was Melisandre's shadowbinding.
rationale: Melisandre performed the shadowbinding ritual that created the shadow assassin, making her the direct executor of the killing even though the shadow was the physical instrument.

evidence_quote: Catelyn 'seizes his arm and swears on Ned's grave and her honor as a Stark that Stannis killed Renly through sorcery'; relationship entry: 'Stannis (shadow) | Kills | Renly | Shadow with immaterial blade cuts through Renly's gorget.'
rationale: Stannis instigated the assassination and his life force was used to create the shadow, but he did not personally execute it.

evidence_quote: Renly says 'Cold' and his gorget parts like cheesecloth; blood gushes from his throat; he collapses into Brienne's arms.
rationale: Renly was killed by the shadow — his gorget parted and blood gushed from his throat.

evidence_quote: Lord Rowan mentions Mace Tyrell's siege [of Storm's End]; the assassination scene takes place in Renly's tent during the military encampment outside Storm's End.
rationale: The assassination occurred inside Renly's tent in his camp near Storm's End.

evidence_quote: Littlefinger slides Ned's dagger from its sheath and holds it under Ned's chin, saying 'I did warn you not to trust me, you know.'
rationale: Ned is the direct target of the arrest, physically seized with a knife held to his throat.

evidence_quote: Cersei orders Ned's arrest — Commands Ser Barristan to seize 'this traitor.'
rationale: Cersei explicitly ordered the arrest without personally executing it, commanding Ser Barristan to seize Ned as a traitor.

evidence_quote: Joffrey screams for killing — 'Kill him! Kill all of them, I command it!'
rationale: Joffrey, as nominal king, issued his own lethal orders reinforcing Cersei's command without personally acting.

evidence_quote: Littlefinger's final betrayal — Slides Ned's dagger from its sheath and holds it under Ned's chin, saying 'I did warn you not to trust me, you know.'
rationale: Littlefinger personally and physically betrayed Ned by drawing a blade against him, directly executing the final act of seizure.

evidence_quote: Varys replies he was unarmed and surrounded by Lannister swords.
rationale: Lannister soldiers physically surrounded and executed the arrest alongside the turned City Watch; Varys confirms Ned was encircled by Lannister swords.

evidence_quote: Arya Stark | Student/devoted to | Syrio Forel | Wants one last lesson, has packed all her things to be ready (killed during the simultaneous sweep against Stark household)
rationale: Syrio Forel was killed defending Arya Stark as part of the same coordinated crackdown that constituted Ned's arrest, making him a victim of the broader event.

evidence_quote: Eddard Stark was arrested in the throne room by Lannister soldiers and the City Watch.
rationale: The arrest took place in the throne room of the Red Keep during the Small Council confrontation.

evidence_quote: Ser Ilyn Payne executes Eddard Stark: Draws Ice and carries out the beheading.
rationale: Eddard Stark was beheaded by Ser Ilyn Payne on Joffrey's order.

evidence_quote: Joffrey commands Ser Ilyn Payne to bring him Eddard's head.
rationale: Joffrey explicitly commanded Ser Ilyn Payne to behead Eddard, overruling both Cersei and Sansa.

evidence_quote: Ser Ilyn Payne: Draws Ice and carries out the beheading.
rationale: Ser Ilyn Payne physically drew Ice and carried out the beheading.

evidence_quote: Ser Ilyn Payne: Draws Ice and carries out the beheading.
rationale: The Stark greatsword Ice was the named weapon Ser Ilyn Payne used to perform the execution.

evidence_quote: Eddard's public confession — Eddard declares himself a traitor [at the Great Sept]; the High Septon kneels before Joffrey and asks him for judgment.
rationale: The execution took place publicly on the steps of the Great Sept of Baelor, where Eddard made his confession.

evidence_quote: Steps from shadows with crossbow; explains his actions are for the realm and children; wants to undo Kevan's stabilizing work
rationale: Varys personally shot Kevan with a crossbow and directed his little-bird child agents to kill Pycelle, then stepped from the shadows to explain his motives.

evidence_quote: Pycelle's head bashed in at his table, blood and brain pooled around him
rationale: Pycelle was found murdered in his own chambers with his skull bashed in before Kevan was shot.

evidence_quote: A crossbow bolt slams into his chest between the ribs
rationale: Kevan was shot in the chest by Varys's crossbow bolt while investigating Pycelle's chambers.

evidence_quote: Aegon raises his banner; monologue about Aegon's fitness to rule; wants to undo Kevan's stabilizing work so doubt and division will eat the ground beneath Tommen
rationale: Varys explicitly frames the murders as serving Aegon's claim, wanting chaos to persist until Aegon raises his banner, though Aegon did not directly order these specific killings.

evidence_quote: Kevan crosses to Pycelle's chambers — Snow has stopped; full moon; castle alien under snow
rationale: Both murders occurred within the Red Keep — Pycelle in his chambers and Kevan after crossing to them through the castle grounds.

evidence_quote: Daenerys acquired the Unsullied by pretending to trade a dragon, then commanded 'Dracarys' — her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied (now freed) killed their former masters.
rationale: Daenerys orchestrated the deception, gave the command 'Dracarys' to her dragons, and ordered the Unsullied to kill their masters, but did not personally execute the killing.

evidence_quote: Grey Worm led the Unsullied assault; Grey Worm offers to batter gates or die in the attempt.
rationale: Grey Worm led the Unsullied assault on Astapor's slavemasters as the direct military executor of Daenerys's command.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers; Drogon breathed fire into empty air.
rationale: Drogon was the primary dragon weapon wielded by Daenerys's command 'Dracarys' to burn Kraznys and the slavers.

evidence_quote: Daenerys acquired the Unsullied by pretending to trade a dragon, then commanded 'Dracarys' — her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers.
rationale: Rhaegal participated in burning Astapor alongside Drogon and Viserion as one of Daenerys's three dragons deployed in the attack.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied killed their former masters.
rationale: Viserion participated in burning Astapor alongside Drogon and Rhaegal as one of Daenerys's three dragons deployed in the attack.

evidence_quote: Dany and Jorah discuss why Astapor has never been sacked; the event is titled the Fall of Astapor.
rationale: The sack took place within Astapor, specifically in the Plaza of Pride and throughout the city.

evidence_quote: The Unsullied (now freed) killed their former masters and took the city.
rationale: The Good Masters were the slavemasters of Astapor who were killed by the Unsullied and burned by the dragons during the sack.

evidence_quote: Her dragons burned Kraznys and the slavers while the Unsullied killed their former masters.
rationale: Kraznys was the first and most prominent victim, burned by Drogon immediately when Daenerys issued the command 'Dracarys'.

evidence_quote: After dark, Dany assembles her bloodriders and captains; plans a surprise attack at an hour past midnight... Dany stays in her pavilion with her maids while Whitebeard and Belwas guard.
rationale: Daenerys planned and ordered the night attack, assigning roles to each force, but remained in her pavilion while her captains executed it.

evidence_quote: He is first of her Queensguard, commander of her army, her most valued counselor.
rationale: As commander of Dany's army he led forces in the field during the battle, distinct from Daenerys who ordered but did not fight.

evidence_quote: Unsullied to strike from right and left; Dothraki horse in wedge through center; Jhogo to deal with enemy scouts.
rationale: Grey Worm personally led the Unsullied in the flanking strikes as explicitly detailed in the attack plan.

evidence_quote: Assigns Stormcrows to attack the Yunkish rear.
rationale: Daario turned the Stormcrows to Dany's side and led them in attacking the Yunkish rear during the battle.

evidence_quote: Dany stays in her pavilion with her maids while Whitebeard and Belwas guard.
rationale: Whitebeard was present with the army but in a rear-guard protective role guarding Dany's pavilion rather than fighting in the main engagement.

evidence_quote: The Second Sons will be drunk on the gifted wine... Mero fled. ~200 Yunkai'i died.
rationale: Mero commanded the Second Sons defending Yunkai; his forces were routed (drunk on the gifted wine) and he himself fled, placing him on the receiving end of the attack.

evidence_quote: Daenerys attacked Yunkai at night with Unsullied, Dothraki, and the turned Stormcrows.
rationale: Yunkai is the city where the battle took place and the strategic target of Daenerys's night assault.

evidence_quote: Robb personally beheaded Karstark despite counsel from Edmure and Catelyn to wait.
rationale: Robb Stark personally swung the sword and beheaded Karstark; because he is simultaneously the orderer and the executor, AGENT_IN subsumes any COMMANDS_IN role — no separate instigator edge is needed.

evidence_quote: Lord Rickard Karstark was executed by Robb Stark for murdering two Lannister hostage boys (Tion Frey and Willem Lannister).
rationale: Rickard Karstark was the condemned man beheaded by Robb for murdering Lannister hostage boys.

evidence_quote: Robb Stark was holding court at Riverrun when Karstark killed the hostages and was subsequently executed there.
rationale: The execution occurred at Riverrun, Robb's base of operations at the time Karstark committed the murders; not directly quoted in the extracted chapter passages but strongly supported by canonical context.

evidence_quote: Tyrion decides to lead the sortie himself — 'This is madness, but sooner madness than defeat. Defeat is death and shame.'
rationale: Personally led the defensive sortie from the Mud Gate and oversaw deployment of wildfire, physically executing the city's defense.

evidence_quote: Tyrion compares Stannis to Lord Tywin — Both command from the reserve/rear; 'Stannis has landed men, Mud Gate under attack.'
rationale: Ordered and instigated the assault on King's Landing, commanding from the reserve/rear rather than personally storming the walls.

evidence_quote: Tyrion compares Stannis to Lord Tywin — Both command from the reserve/rear (additional context: Tywin arrived with Tyrell forces to relieve the city).
rationale: Commanded the Lannister-Tyrell relief force that broke the siege, directing the army from the rear as was his established practice.

evidence_quote: Cersei orders executions — Commands Ser Ilyn to behead the would-be deserters and put their heads on pikes outside the stables.
rationale: Directed internal defense from the Red Keep — ordering executions of deserters and commanding the Kettleblacks — without personally fighting.

evidence_quote: Davos Seaworth led Stannis's fleet (additional context; not directly quoted in chapter excerpts).
rationale: Physically led Stannis's fleet in the naval assault on Blackwater Bay, directly executing the seaborne attack.

evidence_quote: Mace Tyrell reinforced via Renly's former host (additional context; not directly quoted in chapter excerpts).
rationale: Commanded the Tyrell forces drawn from Renly's former host that reinforced the Lannister defense, directing as lord from the rear.

evidence_quote: Ser Loras Tyrell vanguard of relief force (additional context; not directly quoted in chapter excerpts).
rationale: Personally led the vanguard of the Tyrell relief force, physically executing the charge that broke the Baratheon besiegers.

evidence_quote: Tyrion relies on (grudgingly) Ser Mandon Moore — 'Ser Mandon backs him up'; additional context: Ser Mandon Moore attacked Tyrion during the battle.
rationale: Physically rode in the defensive sortie alongside Tyrion and, within the battle, personally attacked Tyrion Lannister.

evidence_quote: 'This is your city Stannis means to sack, and that's your gate he's bringing down'; 'Stannis has landed men, Mud Gate under attack, ram at King's Gate.'
rationale: The battle took place at King's Landing and Blackwater Bay, which was the target of Stannis's assault.
